name: De'Zhaun Stribling
headline: Rookie depth flier in a crowded San Francisco room
body:
San Francisco spent the 33rd overall pick on Stribling, a 6-2, 207-pound Ole Miss product who posted 55 catches, 811 yards and six scores across 15 games last fall. That draft capital signals real long-term interest, and tying his rookie year to Brock Purdy gives the upside a credible ceiling. But the path to early volume is narrow: Mike Evans, Christian Kirk and Ricky Pearsall sit ahead of him on a reshuffled depth chart, leaving him fourth in a competitive pecking order.

The market reflects that logjam, with an expert rank near 229 and an ADP around 165 (67th among receivers). Projections of roughly 32 grabs, 440 yards and two to three touchdowns for about 58 fantasy points fit a depth piece, not a weekly starter. A brutal schedule (30th-toughest, worst-case playoff slate) compounds the risk. Treat him as a cheap dynasty stash with a clearer 2027 payoff than an immediate redraft contributor.
